test_description: For each masked temperature map, subtract the best-fit monopole and dipole on the unmasked sky, then compute the masked pseudo-spectrum \tilde C_l for 2 <= l <= 64 using the same mask for Planck and all simulations. Form the large-angle correlation estimate \tilde C(\theta)=sum_l (2l+1)\tilde C_l P_l(cos theta)/(4pi), and return the single scalar S_{1/2}=integral_{-1}^{1/2} [\tilde C(\theta)]^2 d(cos theta), evaluated numerically or via precomputed Legendre integrals. Low values indicate a deficit of correlations above 60 degrees; assess significance by the empirical rank of the Planck value among the 1000 LCDM simulations.
test_hypothesis: The Planck masked temperature map has an anomalously low large-angle correlation statistic S1/2 compared with ΛCDM simulations, indicating a deficit of correlations at angles above 60 degrees.
justificaton: The test description and code explicitly identify low S1/2 values as the anomaly of interest and compute the empirical significance using the lower tail of the simulation distribution.

custom_summary.test_description: Masked Large-Angle Correlation S1/2: for each masked temperature map, fit and subtract the best-fit monopole plus dipole on the finite unmasked sky, set masked pixels to zero, compute the masked pseudo-C_l spectrum for 2 <= l <= 64, reconstruct C(theta)=sum_l (2l+1) C_l P_l(cos theta)/(4*pi), and return S_{1/2}=int_{-1}^{1/2} C(x)^2 dx. Low values indicate a deficit of correlations above 60 degrees.
custom_summary.tail_of_interest: lower tail: smaller S_{1/2} means less large-angle (theta > 60 deg) temperature correlation in the masked pseudo-C_l estimate
custom_summary.statistic_units: microK^4, with the pseudo-spectrum normalization set by the common mask and zero fill

plot_spec.title: Masked large-angle correlation S1/2
plot_spec.caption: LCDM simulation distribution of S1/2 after the same mask, monopole/dipole subtraction on the unmasked sky, and l=2..64 pseudo-spectrum estimator. The anomaly corresponds to Planck lying in the left tail.

custom_summary.rank_convention: Ascending rank 1 is the smallest S1/2 (largest large-angle-correlation deficit); tie_high includes simulations tied with Planck.
custom_summary.interpretation: Planck lies in the lowest 1% tail of the LCDM simulation S1/2 distribution for this masked large-angle-correlation estimator.

test_summary.Description: Masked Planck and ΛCDM simulation temperature maps were monopole/dipole-subtracted on the unmasked sky, converted to masked pseudo-C_l for 2≤l≤64, and integrated as S_{1/2}=∫_{-1}^{1/2} C(θ)^2 dcosθ. Low values test for deficient correlations above 60°.
test_summary.Results: Planck S_{1/2}=1320.97 μK^4 versus simulation mean 36187.98 μK^4 and median 18176.00 μK^4. Only 6/1000 simulations were lower, giving one-tailed p=0.006, empirical plus-one p≈0.00699, and σ≈−2.51.
test_summary.Interpretation: Planck lies in the lowest ≈1% of the ΛCDM simulation distribution for this masked estimator, indicating a statistically significant deficit of large-angle temperature correlations.
test_summary.Comparison to literature: This is consistent with the long-reported low large-angle CMB correlation / S_{1/2} anomaly in WMAP and Planck analyses. Planck 2018 “Isotropy and Statistics of the CMB” confirms persistence of large-scale temperature anomalies including lack of large-angle correlation; see Planck Collaboration VII, arXiv:1906.02552.
